vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The biome is in a state of advanced degradation. P1 (String of Nickels), P2 (Mexican Mint), P3 (Pothos), and P4 (Silver Guest) are not visually identifiable as living specimens. The pots contain only soil and debris.
vision_report.inventory_reconciliation: Systemic Loss: All registered plants (P1, P2, P3, P4) are absent or have perished. The white rabbit anchor is present but the associated P3 Pothos is not.

plant_audit.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.
plant_audit.P3: Systemic Loss: Only the white rabbit anchor remains; no Pothos foliage visible.
plant_audit.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter detected.

vision_report.biome_observations: The soil surface appears consistently dark and devoid of organic growth. No moss, seedlings, or sprouts are present. The desk surface shows no new debris, though lighting conditions have shifted to a low-spectrum, high-contrast state in the final frames.
vision_report.temporal_deltas: From Earliest to Current: The sequence shows a transition from a state of potential dormancy to total absence of visible plant life. The lighting shift in the final two images obscures the soil surface, but no structural recovery or growth is observed in the preceding clear frames.
vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is currently non-viable. The absence of chlorophyll-bearing tissue across all pots indicates a total loss of the botanical subjects.
vision_report.anomalies: The presence of the white rabbit anchor in an empty pot (P3) is the primary anomaly. The shift to low-light/blue-spectrum imaging in the final frames suggests a potential hardware or environmental lighting failure.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit confirms a complete systemic failure of the botanical collection. The pots are empty, and the biological components have been lost. The environment is currently static and devoid of life.
